reforms.description: A legislative proposal is being prepared to switch from grants to a loan-based support system for living costs for students, raising the maximum guaranteed loan amount and introducing a partial tax allowance for timely course completion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance the financial support for students, potentially increasing their ability to complete education and contribute to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal is currently being prepared and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the support system for students, moving from grants to loans.
reforms.source_quote: The recent Employment Working Group proposed a switch from grants to a loan-based support system for living costs, making more funding available in the early years of study while strengthening the incentives to complete studies on time.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has launched a broad programme aimed at raising public sector productivity through administrative simplifications, better use of IT, and increased competition in service provision.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme is designed to enhance efficiency and productivity in the public sector, which can lead to better service delivery and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been officially launched but details on implementation are not provided.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in how public services are delivered, aiming for significant improvements in productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched a broad programme aiming at administrative simplifications, better use of IT and increased exposure to competition.
